title: vernonia cinerea var. vialis
intro: Vernonia cinerea var. vialis, also known as White Ironweed, is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Vernonia cinerea var. vialis include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as being used as a diuretic and expectorant. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent, and are often used to treat various ailments in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
